calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Relieving Petitioners From The Provisions Of Government Code Section 954.4
Off Calendar Nov-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONERS RICHARD "BRAC" SELPH, ELIZABETH SELPH, CATHRINE JACOBSON and DAVID SELPH'S Hearing On Petition For Order Relieving Petitioners From The Provisions Of Government Code Section 954.4.

The "petition for order relieving petitioners from the provisions of Government Code section 954.4 [sic]" is granted.

Petitioners - adult children of Richard Selph - allege that dangerous road conditions on San Francisco's Market Street caused him to lose control of a motor scooter and crash on December 30, 2020. He died January 19, 2021. On May 18, 2021, Selph's widow filed a timely claim against the City and County of San Francisco. However, the four petitioners failed to file claims before the six-month deadline of Government Code Sec. 911.2. This was due, it is said, to petitioners' attorney's clerical error. On September 8, 2021, petitioners applied for leave to file late claims - applications the City rejected.

I assume petitioners mean to seek relief from Government Code Sec. 945.4, not from a nonexistent Sec. 954.4 (to be fair, the City's opposition makes the same error). The parties agree that relief from late presentation of claims is provided by Government Code Sec. 946.6 and three factors are key: whether (1) petitioners' request for relief was within a reasonable time, (2) lateness was due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect and (3) the City would be prejudiced.

The parties further agree that the "reasonable time" of Sec. 946.6 is between realization of the mistake and filing for relief; here, that is only seven days. Counsel shows the mistake was due to setting up an office file only for the widow and not for the four children. (Emison Dec. 3:11-4:1.) The City says it was "robbed of prompt notice" and the ability to "plan its budget," but this will be true of essentially all late claim presentations. Moreover, our supreme court has repeatedly directed that late claim relief be granted liberally. (Bettencourt v. Los Rios Community College Dist. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 270, 275 ("favor of relief whenever possible"); Ebersol v. Cowan (1983) 35 Cal.3d 427, 435 ("wherever possible cases be heard on their merits").)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
